from.subject: Emlid Tech: Incorrect shipping rates at PH app

ticket.subject: Re: We haven't heard from you since 48 hours
ticket.raw_subject: Re: We haven't heard from you since 48 hours
ticket.description:
This is a follow-up to your previous request #353351 "Emlid Tech: Incorrect shipp..."

Hi Sagar,

I have placed a test order #1413
Packages split does not work for me: - instead of 3 boxes I am getting 2 boxes (pls see screenshot below)
Also, even for 8 RCH205-I which were allocated in a separate box, the box chosen is "Generic (config 2)" instead of dedicated RS3 box that should have been chosen - pls investigate the reason behind it

Also,  you have mentioned that you are using pounds and inches in your demo store. Please note that in our store we use kg and cm. I would suggest that you can use the same measures in your demo store to make this testing true to life and eliminate probability of any mistakes

Lastly, I would like to suggest to revisit our request to make insurance calculation correct even if package was changed.
The reason is that we have a lot of shipping scenarios and I am sure that mistakes with box allocation in PH app will still happen , thus, we will still need to correct packages manually. If we correct package, then insurance is not calculated correctly. However, I assume that it requires just some minor changes to backend code to tell the system that even if package change has occurred, declared value that is passed to UPS should be still discounted value (35% of the product value )

Thank you!




Kind regards,

--

Valeria Kolobova

Sales Support Team Lead

Emlid (https://emlid.com/)

comments.body:
Hi Sagar,



I have placed a test order #1413

Packages split does not work for me: - instead of 3 boxes I am getting 2 boxes (pls see screenshot below)

Also, even for 8 RCH205-I which were allocated in a separate box, the box chosen is "Generic (config 2)" instead of dedicated RS3 box that should have been chosen - pls investigate the reason behind it



Also,  you have mentioned that you are using pounds and inches in your demo store. Please note that in our store we use **kg and cm.** I would suggest that you can use the same measures in your demo store to make this testing true to life and eliminate probability of any mistakes



Lastly, I would like to suggest to revisit our request to **make insurance calculation correct even if package was changed.**

The reason is that we have a lot of shipping scenarios and I am sure that mistakes with box allocation in PH app will still happen , thus, we will still need to correct packages manually. If we correct package, then insurance is not calculated correctly. However, I assume that it requires just some minor changes to backend code to tell the system that even if package change has occurred, declared value that is passed to UPS should be still discounted value (35% of the product value )



Thank you!



 ![Screenshot 2025-11-27 at 12.11.45.png](https://support.pluginhive.com/attachments/token/ma6xdxKeTsPrTlcSNF5glMrWe/?name=Screenshot+2025-11-27+at+12.11.45.png)





Kind regards,

--

Valeria Kolobova

Sales Support Team Lead

Emlid

Hi Sagar,

I have placed a test order #1413
Packages split does not work for me: - instead of 3 boxes I am getting 2 boxes (pls see screenshot below)
Also, even for 8 RCH205-I which were allocated in a separate box, the box chosen is "Generic (config 2)" instead of dedicated RS3 box that should have been chosen - pls investigate the reason behind it

Also,  you have mentioned that you are using pounds and inches in your demo store. Please note that in our store we use kg and cm. I would suggest that you can use the same measures in your demo store to make this testing true to life and eliminate probability of any mistakes

Lastly, I would like to suggest to revisit our request to make insurance calculation correct even if package was changed.
The reason is that we have a lot of shipping scenarios and I am sure that mistakes with box allocation in PH app will still happen , thus, we will still need to correct packages manually. If we correct package, then insurance is not calculated correctly. However, I assume that it requires just some minor changes to backend code to tell the system that even if package change has occurred, declared value that is passed to UPS should be still discounted value (35% of the product value )

Thank you!




Kind regards,

--

Valeria Kolobova

Sales Support Team Lead

Emlid

L3 - The customer case is regarding discounted product price provided under App Settings -> General Settings -> Shipping, is not getting applied for the products when the package is manually edited.

For example - The merchant is selling a product A with a price of $100 and for the end customer on the checkout, the merchant is giving 50% discount which comes to $50 when the order is placed.

Now, under the App Settings -> General Settings -> Shipping -> Customs/Insured Amount To Be Used -> Percentage of Product Price is set as 35%.

So, for the declared value, when the automatic packaging is happening, 35% of $50 = $17.5 should be considered which is working fine. However, when the package is manually edited, the 35% is getting considered for $100 and not $50.

We assisted the customer to setup the package details and product details in such a way that the packaging split among the products will happen automatically, however, the customer is not convinced with the current packaging happened and they have multiple times informed that manual intervention for the package splitting is required.

I have recorded a video showing the case with details tested in our demo store, and have attached the recording for reference.

Could you please check about he case and provide an update?

App - Shopify MCSL
URL - b2b-portal-eu.myshopify.com
Netherlands store and UPS account added
Order Count - 45
15 labels in Nov
Active from Aug 2025 and $19 plan selected

comments.body:
Hello Valeria,

Good Day!

Upon checking about your requirement, i would like to provide the details of my observation and how the box packing method works in our app.

For the order 1413, the app is considering two boxes to pack the products. The Generic (Config 1) box is considered to pack all 6 quantities of SPL102 and 8 quantities of RCH205-I. The remaining 8 quantities of RCH205-I are getting packed in Generic (Config 2) box.

This is because, the app uses an algorithm to pack the products optimally and looks to reduce the wastage of the space.

Also, the following criteria is considered by the app during the packing of the products and selection of a suitable box.


1. The length, width, and height of the product are compared **individually** to the length, width, and height of the box.
2. Total Volume of all the items in the order must be less than the box's volume.
3. Total Weight of all items in the order should be less than the MaxWeight limit of the box.
4. If the "Stack" option is enabled, the height of all products is summed up to determine the best-fitting box.

Boundary conditions like rotating the product are not considered in the box packing method.
**To get a better understanding of how the box packing strategy works with our app, please refer to this video:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Cdt2hqF6wRU

For the current order, the app see that only two boxes can be used for the packaging instead of 3 boxes considering all the above mentioned criteria.

Also, the app is considering the Generic (Config 1) box and the Generic (Config 2) box for packaging due to the following.


- The app see that 6 quantities of SPL102 and 8 quantities of RCH205-I can be packed in Config 1 box as this box can hold upto 24.9 kg and the total weight of 6 quantities of SPL102 and 8 quantities of RCH205-I is 24.86 kg.

- The app then considered the Config 2 box as this box size and volume is lesser than that of the box 8 x RS2+/RS3 and also can hold remaining 8 quantities.

Please note that it is not possible to pack a certain product into a certain box automatically at all times as the package settings is global and works based on all the above criteria mentioned and also considering less wastage of space.

So, for your specific requirements, we recommend to consider package editing for the orders.

Thanks and Regards,
Sagar K S
Team PluginHive
